advice to a daughter . V. religion . 1. read the book of God with reverence , and in thing doubtful take fixtion from the authority of the church , which can be arraign of a damnable error , without question that truth which have proclaim her proof against the gate of hell , etc i answer ; God have leave three book to the world in , each of which he may easy be find ; the book of the creature , the book of conscience , and his write word : the first show his omnipotency , the second his justice , the three his mercy and goodness ; so though there be none of they so barren of the rudiment of knowledge , but be sufficient to leave all without excuse , and apology : yet in they all , you find all the good that ever either the heathen , or the christian have publish abroad : in the first be all natural philosophy : in the second all moral philosophy : in the three all true divinity , to those admirable pillar of all humane learning , the philosopher God show himself in his omnipotency and justice , but seem as it be to conceal his mercy ; to Christians he shine in that which outshine all his work , his mercy : oh , how shall we regratulate his favour for so immense a benefit , wherein seclude himself from other , he have whole impart himself to we ! in the first of these , Brightman be not out , nor will i have you , but to admire his work , by a serious meditation of the wonder in the creature . in the second i will have you reverence his justice by the secret and in most check of the conscience : in the three embrace his love by lay hold on those promise , wherein he have not only leave you mean to know he , but to love he , rest in he , and enjoy he forever . 2. the prudent consistory find , etc i answer ; can a fly comprehend man upon the top of monarchy ? no more can you comprehend God in the height of omnipotency ; there be as well mystery for faith , as cause for reason : this may guide you , when you have to deal with man ; but in divine affair , reason must wait on faith , and submit to her prerogative . the conscience be great , but God be far great than it . 3. he may be less prudent , etc i do not think the great clerk be near heaven , much of their knowledge be superfluous ; for Bellarmine make four hundred question of faith : and doctor Owen make a gracious tale of a great many more ; and not ten of they which touch our salvation to understand . 4. despise not a profession of holiness , etc i answer ; word be not the difference of good man and bad ; for every man speak well : therefore how noble a thing be virtue , when no man dare profess any thing elss ? 5. hypocrisy though look upon , etc i answer ; as i think there be many worse than they seem , so i suppose there be some better than they show , and these be like the grow Chesnut that keep a sweet and nutrimental kernel include in a rough and prickly husk : the other as the peach ; hold a rugged and craggy stone under the cover of a velvet coat : you shall not deceive a good man either way , both offer a wrong to virtue ; the one show she worse than she be , dull her beauty with dim colour , and present she with a hard favour than her own ; the other do varnish over the rottenness of vice , and make goodness but the vizor of hypocrisy , either be condemnable : paint the face be not much worse than wilful soil it ; he be as well a murderer , that accuse himself false , as he that do the act , and deny it . one will obscure goodness with vice , the other will palliate vice with goodness , fraud be in both ; and i be sure no pleasure can make deceit allowable : you must therefore strive to avoid both , and with either seem as you be , or be as you seem ; but if you will err on one side , i have rather you shall resemble a plain country man , that go in russet , and be rich in revenue , than a riotous courtier that wear glorious apparel without money in his purse . 6. criticism and curious question . etc i answer ; if a man shall once take upon he to call that light which God call heavy , that sin venial , which God call grievous , measure any one sin by the measure of his lust and appetite , and not of his conscience : what shall let he to do with the next that his affection stir he to , ( the like reason serve for all ) and so go forward till he place his whole corrupt affection in God room . 7. i can approve of none for , etc , i can approve of none for , etc i answer ; God who call his elect unto himself to make they enjoy heaven , compel none to make defection from himself : beside , the devil never assail a magisterial divine ; i mean a man so call , except he find he either void of knowledge , the fear of God , or in controversy with his word . 8. i grant the socinians be not , etc i answer ; that in the direct worship of God himself , you ought to be guide by the word of God , as he prescribe in the same , and not otherwise , as also in the matter of sacrifice ; but in the form and order of ceremony , that indeed be sole leave unto the church , but not the immediate worship , we may not therein follow our own will , that be the main difference between they and the ancient canon of the church , if you may use a will-worship , than you be in the right with they , but if you may not , than you be in the right with we . 8. and as the Socinian doctrine , etc i answer ; the devil where he can have the whole , seek ever to get one part of the soul , either the will or the understanding , which he may come easy by ; as in protestant the will , in socinians then understand : a learned Socinian , and a ignorant , be of two religion . 10 , yet be not purgatory , etc i answer ; the papist religion be like Homer Iliad of the siege of Troy , or virgils aenead of the beginning of roam ; both of they have a foundation of truth ; so have the papist the bible , but they have all add so much that the first truth be almost lose . 11. but in conclusion you will find , etc i answer ; if the pope may err as a man , but not as a pope , i will know why the pope do not instruct or reform the man , or wherefore the man do not require the pope instruction ? 12. and yet it be no unhappy , etc i answer ; it must needs show the Puritan and papist religion to be both ill , that they will plant they by liberty of conscience and war ; whereas the true protestant religion rise by fast and prayer , and be set up upon the rock of faith , for every good work to mount up the soul to eternal rest with God . 13. i confess the millenary , etc i answer ; the true protestant religion stand like a virtue between the presbyterian , papist , Annabaptist , independent , Quaker , Millenary ; that be , a extremity in the excess , this part of they in the effect ; that aim at the confusion of the state , this other make confusion in the church : let that prince that desire the welfare of his kingdom , crush the power of the first part , and curb the malice of the other , so shall his church be peaceful , his state honourable , and on his head shall his crown flourish . 14. but for the vagabond , etc i answer ;
